sections.title: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act

sections.title: On specific functionalities in digital products
path: On specific functionalities in digital products

paragraphs.text: AN3
NATIONAL AUTHORITY DES GAMES

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: The Autorité Nationale des Jeux (ANJ) is an independent administrative authority responsible for regulating gambling in France. It has four main tasks: preventing excessive gambling and ensuring the protection of minors, ensuring the integrity of gambling operations, ensuring a balance between the various gaming channels, and preventing fraudulent activities.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: The ANJ oversees a wide perimeter, covering all monopoly and competing players on all types of licensed online and hard games (lotteries, horse-race betting, sports betting, poker). The ANJ is also responsible for supervising land-based casinos on the aspects relating to the prevention of excessive gambling and the protection of minors.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: The ANJ, which aims in particular to protect players/consumers, supports the general objective of strengthening the protection of consumers, but also of minors, against their exposure to gambling.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: It stresses the fact that gambling is an area regulated in each State by national law and it is important to maintain this specificity in the light of the specific social challenges in question.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: However, with regard to consumer protection, the ANJ recognises that the current European legal framework, coupled with national gambling laws, is useful and effective.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: The ANJ considers that this framework is sufficient in relation to the issue of digital fairness, with the exception of the challenges relating to certain specific functionalities present in digital products, for which the ANJ would support targeted action at European level.

section_path: Position of the National Games Authority (ANJ) on the Digital Fairness Act
paragraphs.text: Finally, the ANJ wishes to point out that there is no need for European rules on the protection of consumers against illegal gambling offers.

In the field of gambling, the question of "digital fairness" arises especially with regard to the development of online games and the advertising of operators who relay their gambling offers on social media, possibly through partnerships with influencers. Many practices, particularly affecting young people, have been deplored in the past.
A regulator serving a safe, integrated and controlled game
TRIEO Building – 11 Boulevard Gallieni, 92130 Issy-les-Moulineaux, France – Tel.: +33 (0) 1 57 13 13 00 - www.anj.fr
ANJ NATIONAL AUTHORITY OES GAMES

In recent years, however, the already existing European legal and regulatory tools have been able to be used and, together with the national provisions applicable to gambling, have proved to be sufficient.

First, French law (via the Eta Counciland 1 then NAJ2) confirmed that Directive 2005/29/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 May 2005 concerning unfair business-to-consumer commercial practices in the internal market can be applied to most of the processes used by gambling operators.

Secondly, French gambling law complements the application of the Directive by strictly regulating the presentation and operation of operators’ sites (including the obligation to use the French language, the making available of conditions of use, etc.)3.

Finally, faced with the development of influencers, the French law of 9 June 2023 made France the first European State to establish a specific legal framework regulating the activity of commercial influence by introducing several major innovations in its legislative landscape. In particular, for gambling, the Act allows influence only on online platforms that have the technical capacity to exclude access to minors and have effectively activated the exclusion mechanism.
In conclusion, it appears that in the field of gambling, the existing European regulatory tools, coupled with national gambling measures, are numerous, effective and sufficient.

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: The ANJ shares many of the options envisaged by the Commission in its questionnaire. Already in its annual report of 2017-20184, the ANJ warned about the phenomenon of microtransactions and surprises in video games, accessible to minors, which is an apprenticeship for betting and slot machines. Literature, rather

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: ANJ

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: emerging so far, points out that these practices reflect a convergence between video and argent games5.

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: The Authority therefore supports action at European level on these aspects, including certain proposals made by the Commission in its questionnaire, namely to oblige the operators concerned to:

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: express in legal tender currency the price of embedded purchases offered in exchange for paying virtual currencies;

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: ensure greater transparency of win-probabilities when purchasing virtual items with random rewards;

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: prohibit certain functionalities for minors, including (but not limited to) paid surprises boxes;

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: to allow the player to disable paid surprises boxes, the purchase of card packs or combat passes 6 (the latter may, moreover, be more broadly regulated).

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: The ANJ would also like to point out that this draft regulation could incorporate mechanisms of moderation among players in the aforementioned digital functionalities in order to prevent excessive gambling behaviour. Such arrangements are currently mandatory in France for gambling operators regulated by the ANJ (for example, the obligation for a player registering on the website of a gambling operator to set a maximum deposit and stakes limit or the possibility for the latter to exclude himself for a fixed period).

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: Finally, the ANJ considers that the practices identified by the Commission in relation to video games are even more worrying when they are offered to the player in a 'web3' video game, that is to say, based on blockchain technology. In those worlds, players obtain as rewards not only virtual currencies or assets internal to gaming, but also assets with a real value, such as NFTs or cryptoassets, which they may lawfully resell, directly or indirectly, in return for other crypto-assets or even the currency which is legal tender, without those gains ultimately being captive to the gaming ecosystem and the publisher. It is in this context that France has experimentally introduced a framework for regulating 'monetised digital games' (JONUM) to regulate this type ofoffer7.

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: While the ANJ concedes that consumers are exposed to misleading commercial practices through the illegal gambling offer, and also to the development of illegal gambling offers around the video game ecosystem (in particular the development of illegal online casino sites to sell or win CSGO2 cosmetics distributed on Steam ('skins'), the ANJ would also like to clarify that efficient national control tools already exist: for example, in France, the ANJ practices the blocking of websites, which makes it impossible to access access from its territory of 1300 URLs on average pern8.

section_path: On specific functionalities in digital products
paragraphs.text: The ANJ therefore considers that there is no need for additional EU rules in this area, especially since cooperation between European gambling regulators is already working well with constant exchanges of information and best practices on this subject.
